mission.title: thanos: Ability to require tenant in queries
mission.description: Ability to require tenant in queries. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current thanos deployment
steps.description:
Verify your thanos version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
helm list -n thanos 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working thanos installation.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
### Is your proposal related to a problem?

The most questions we get about Thanos are around multi tenancy, and I think it's worth considering that we could have more first class multi tenancy features. This is about the query path.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to require tenant in queries
steps.description:
Fixes [#3822](https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/issues/3822) Related to [Proposal](https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/pull/4055)
We take the tenant header and replace the tenant label if specified in the query, if not just append it in prometheus label matches using [enforceMatcher](https://github.com/prometheus-community/prom-label-proxy/blob/38b1382d7368e994e39c0cb853f3f3c9300f5045/injectproxy/enforce.go#L114)(helper function defined in prom-label proxy). Then these labels are further used

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to require tenant in queries" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fixes [#3822](https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/issues/3822) Related to [Proposal](https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/pull/4055)
We take the tenant header and replace the tenant label if specified in the query, if not just append it in prometheus label matches using

See the source issue and linked PR for the complete solution details.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Thanos deployed (sidecar, query, store gateway, compactor as needed). The thanos binary provides subcommands for each component.
